one_time_description: First Nations Finance Authority Series 2020-2 Revenue Bond issuance of $37.6M used to fund a promissory note to First Nations Fisheries Interest Group Partnership; major non-recurring financing transaction
economic_notes: COVID-19 caused significant decrease in commercial fisheries revenues and gaming operation remained closed for majority of year. Band Council owns interests in Mi'kmaq Wind4all Communities Limited Partnership and Beaubassin Mi'kmaq Wind Management Ltd Solar Photovoltaic System. Issued $37.6M FNFA bond and loaned proceeds to First Nations Fisheries Interest Group Partnership at 6.14%.
notes: Consolidated entities include Pictou Landing Band Council, Pictou Landing Housing Fund, A'Se'K Seven Generations Trust, and Victoria Corner Market (4 entities). Major non-recurring transaction: $37.6M FNFA Revenue Bond issued and loaned to First Nations Fisheries Interest Group Partnership via promissory note at 6.14% maturing 2029. This significantly inflated both long-term debt and financial assets. Demand loans of $10.1M also exist separately from long-term debt. Purchased fishing licenses of $5.4M recorded as intangible non-financial assets not included in TCA. Annual debt service calculated as repayment of long-term debt $199,090 plus repayment of demand debt $1,071,201 plus repayment of long-term payable $56,000 = $1,326,291. No schedule of remuneration included in the document. A'Se'K Seven Generations Trust revenue includes unrealized investment gains. Prior year government transfers per Note 16 total $12,763,065 but income statement shows same figure. Mi'kmaw Kina'matnewey prior year was $5,279,503 vs $1,465,583 in 2021, a major decrease.
